subject: Update DCAE certs for El Alto
commit_message:
Update DCAE certs for El Alto

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2206
Signed-off-by: Jack Lucas <jflucas@research.att.com>
Change-Id: I3ddc4b8226a65a57fcb1b289e6f7a472b35032cc

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

____________________________________________________________

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

DCAE HC are failing sorry

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> DCAE HC are failing sorry

The healthcheck is failing because there is some problem with dcae-cloudify-manager.  Unfortunately the relevant logs are not written to stderr/stdout, so cannot tell why.  From the dcae-bootstrap-manager log, it is clear that dcae-cloudify-manager was working properly until suddenly it began returning "500 Server Error" responses to API requests.

There is nothing I can change in this submission to try to make a fix.  I suspect it's a transient problem, since I had no issues in my local environment.   The only change here is to update certificates.  There is no change to any logic and no change to dcae-cloudify-manager (other than that it got a new TLS certificate, which worked properly for many API requests).

Patch Set 1:

Let's try again then. oom_redeploy

Patch Set 1:

In this case, the dcae-servicechange-handler never got past the initialization stage because the dmaap-message-router did not become available.

It appears, though, that the bootstrap process is also encountering error responses from the dcae-cloudify-manager API, as it did in the previous gating deployment.   I cannot reproduce this in my local environment.  There are clearly other problems with the gating deployment, but they don't appear to be related to dcae-cloudify-manager in any way.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1
